name: xAdvCool
description: A benchmark dataset of 3D conjugate heat transfer (CHT) simulations for data-driven surrogate modeling of cold plate heat sinks. Contains Lattice Boltzmann simulations across 5 topology types (Straight Channels, Pin-Fin Staggered, Gyroid TPMS, Schwarz P TPMS, Schwarz D TPMS) and 10 heat source patterns, with water cooling through copper cold plates. Each sample maps a geometry mask and operating conditions to steady-state velocity, pressure, and temperature fields on a 148x128x32 lattice.
citation: Gautam, T. (2026). xAdvCool: A Benchmark Dataset for 3D Conjugate Heat Transfer Surrogate Modeling. NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks Track.
rai:dataLimitations: All quantities are stored in non-dimensional lattice Boltzmann (LBM) units; conversion to SI requires the user to supply reference scales (lattice spacing, time step, reference density, viscosity, thermal diffusivity).
Simulations are performed on a fixed 148x128x32 lattice. Geometric features smaller than ~2 lattice cells are not faithfully resolved, and resolution-dependent errors are not quantified per-sample.

The flow regime is restricted to laminar/transitional Reynolds numbers attainable by single-relaxation-time BGK LBM (no turbulence model is used). Results outside the validated parameter range may be inaccurate.
Conjugate heat transfer is solved with constant material properties (fixed solid/fluid thermal diffusivity ratio) and no buoyancy, phase change, radiation, or temperature-dependent viscosity.

Boundary conditions are idealized: uniform inlet velocity, zero-gradient outlet, no-slip on solid walls, and adiabatic outer walls. Real-world cold-plate effects (manifold maldistribution, thermal interface resistance, gasket leakage) are not modeled.
Steady-state convergence is declared via a residual threshold; samples flagged converged=false should be filtered out for benchmarking. A small fraction of samples may have residual transient error.
Coverage is limited to 5 topology families and 10 heat-source patterns. Generalization to unseen topology families or arbitrary heat maps is not guaranteed and is itself a benchmark task (Task C).
The dataset contains 1,090 samples — small by modern ML standards. Data-hungry models (e.g., large transformers) may overfit without strong regularization or pretraining.
rai:dataBiases: Geometry classes are not perfectly balanced (Straight Channels: 264, Pin-Fin Staggered: 175, Gyroid TPMS: 205, Schwarz P TPMS: 191, Schwarz D TPMS: 255). Models trained without class balancing may favor over-represented topologies.

Operating-condition sampling (inlet velocity, heat power, geometric feature size and spacing) is drawn from grids/uniform ranges chosen by the dataset author. Real cold-plate operating distributions in industry may differ.

All simulations use a single solver (custom JAX/CUDA LBM implementation) with one numerical scheme (D3Q19 BGK + D3Q7 advection-diffusion). Solver-specific numerical artifacts are present in every sample and constitute a systematic bias.
Heat-source patterns are stylized abstractions of electronic-component layouts (CPU, GPU, IGBT, SoC) rather than measured power maps from real devices.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The dataset contains only synthetic numerical fields from physics simulations and contains no personal, sensitive, or human-subject data.
rai:dataUseCases: Benchmarking neural surrogate models (CNN, U-Net, FNO, DeepONet, ViT, MeshGraphNet) for 3D conjugate heat transfer field prediction.
Studying generalization across heat-sink topologies (in-distribution geometry, out-of-distribution geometry, and unseen topology families).
Evaluating physics-informed and operator-learning methods on a non-trivial coupled momentum/energy problem with solid-fluid interfaces.
Pretraining or fine-tuning models for downstream cold-plate / electronics-cooling design optimization and inverse design tasks.
Educational use: teaching CFD-ML integration, scientific machine learning, and surrogate modeling.
rai:dataSocialImpact: The dataset is intended to accelerate the design of efficient liquid cooling solutions for data centers and power electronics, where thermal management is a major contributor to energy consumption. Faster, more accurate surrogate models can reduce the compute and time cost of CFD-based design iteration, lowering the carbon footprint of hardware development. Risks of misuse are minimal: the data describes generic heat-sink physics and contains no personal, proprietary, or dual-use information. A possible negative externality is the energy used to run the underlying LBM simulations and to train models on this dataset; users are encouraged to report compute usage transparently.

rai:dataSources.name: xAdvCool generation code (seed)
rai:dataSources.description: All samples are synthetic and produced by the xAdvCool LBM simulation pipeline. There are no upstream source datasets; the 'seed' for each sample is a parametric specification consisting of (geometry topology family, geometric feature_size and spacing, heat-source pattern name, inlet velocity, heat power, BGK relaxation time tau_fluid). These specifications are stored per-sample in metadata.parquet and are sufficient — together with the generation code — to fully regenerate the dataset.

rai:dataCollection: All 1,090 samples are produced by direct numerical simulation; no human collection, scraping, or third-party data ingestion is involved. For each sample, a parametric geometry is generated (signed-distance functions for TPMS lattices; analytical primitives for straight-channel and pin-fin layouts), voxelized onto a 148x128x32 lattice, and assigned a heat-source pattern from a fixed library of 10 power maps. A custom JAX/CUDA Lattice Boltzmann solver advances the coupled momentum (D3Q19 BGK) and energy (D3Q7 advection-diffusion) equations until a residual-based steady-state criterion is met. The resulting velocity, pressure, temperature, mask, and heat-source fields are written to data.h5; scalar parameters and post-processed performance metrics (Re, Nu, R_th, f_friction, COP, convergence flag) are written to metadata.parquet.
rai:dataCollectionType: Synthetic data generation via numerical simulation (Lattice Boltzmann CFD with conjugate heat transfer).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Post-processing: dimensionless performance metrics (Reynolds number Re based on hydraulic diameter, Nusselt number Nu, thermal resistance R_th, Darcy friction factor f_friction, coefficient of performance COP) are computed from the steady-state fields and stored alongside scalar inputs in metadata.parquet.

Convergence filtering: each sample is tagged with a boolean 'converged' flag based on a residual threshold on velocity, pressure, and temperature fields. Users are expected to filter on converged=true for benchmarking; non-converged samples are retained for transparency.

Validation: 10 analytical CHT/CFD benchmarks (Poiseuille flow, conduction in slab, convection-diffusion, etc.) are run against the same solver; agreement is within 3-5% error. Results of this validation are reported in the accompanying paper but the validation samples themselves are not included in the released dataset.
Storage: 3D fields are written as float32 (and int8 for the geometry mask) into a single HDF5 file with one group per sample_id; scalars are written to a Parquet index keyed by sample_id.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Not applicable. All ground-truth fields and scalar metrics are produced deterministically by the simulator and post-processing pipeline; there is no human labeling, crowdsourcing, or LLM-based annotation step.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: xAdvCool LBM simulation pipeline
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Each sample was generated by a custom Lattice Boltzmann Method solver implemented in JAX with CUDA acceleration. The solver couples a D3Q19 BGK scheme for incompressible flow with a D3Q7 advection-diffusion scheme for temperature, and uses a halfway bounce-back boundary condition at solid-fluid interfaces for conjugate heat transfer. Geometries are generated parametrically (signed-distance functions for TPMS surfaces, analytical primitives for straight-channel and pin-fin layouts) and voxelized onto a 148x128x32 lattice. Heat-source patterns are stylized power maps placed on the substrate face. Each simulation is run to steady state under a residual-based convergence criterion; non-dimensional performance metrics (Re, Nu, R_th, f_friction, COP) are computed in post-processing. The full generation code is available alongside the dataset.

prov:used.name: Parametric geometry generator (Straight Channels, Pin-Fin Staggered, Gyroid TPMS, Schwarz P TPMS, Schwarz D TPMS)
Heat-source pattern library (10 patterns: uniform, dual_core, quad_core, gpu_die, chiplet, igbt_half_bridge, soc_heterogeneous, gaussian_hotspots, hotspot_on_background, peripheral_ring)

distribution.name: data.h5
distribution.description: HDF5 file containing all simulation samples. Each top-level group is a sample keyed by sample_id, containing 5 datasets (mask, heat_source, velocity, pressure, temperature) and scalar attributes.

distribution.name: metadata.parquet
distribution.description: Parquet metadata index with scalar parameters and performance metrics for each sample.

recordSet.name: samples
recordSet.description: Individual CHT simulation samples. 3D field data is stored in the HDF5 file; scalar metadata is in the Parquet file. Linked by sample_id.

field.name: sample_id
field.description: Unique sample identifier (also the HDF5 group key)

field.name: design_name
field.description: Geometry topology type: Straight Channels, Pin-Fin Staggered, Gyroid TPMS, Schwarz P TPMS, or Schwarz D TPMS

field.name: heat_source_type
field.description: Heat source pattern name (e.g., uniform, dual_core, gpu_die)

field.name: tau_fluid
field.description: BGK relaxation time for fluid (controls viscosity: nu = (tau - 0.5)/3)

field.name: Re
field.description: Reynolds number based on hydraulic diameter

field.name: COP
field.description: Coefficient of Performance (thermal performance / pumping cost)

field.name: converged
field.description: Whether the simulation reached steady-state convergence
